conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I still haven't unpacked the last box from the move.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My sister's flight got delayed by a couple of hours.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: AliasIndex only matches exact concept-name strings right now, so 'Manager AI' and 'the manager' never resolve to the same concept.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The office plants finally got watered after being neglected for weeks.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Could just require users to always type the exact canonical concept name.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I keep meaning to update my resume but haven't gotten around to it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: That's not realistic for a personal-memory system -- add a small alias table plus fuzzy matching so near-miss phrasings still resolve.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The parking garage near the office is under construction again.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I tried a new recipe for dinner last night, turned out decent.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Constraint: fuzzy matching must never merge two genuinely different concepts just because their names are similar -- false merges are worse than missed matches.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My laptop fan has been louder than usual, might need cleaning.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Blocked: with fuzzy matching on, ambiguous short aliases (like 'MA') start matching multiple concepts with no way to pick.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The dentist appointment I've been putting off is finally scheduled.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to return that package before the return window closes.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Decided: when an alias matches multiple concepts, score candidates by confirmation count and co-mention frequency and take the highest, rather than resolving arbitrarily. Unblocks alias resolution.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The wifi in the coffee shop I like to work from has been spotty.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Implemented the alias table alongside exact matching in AliasIndex.rebuild.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I finally got around to donating some old clothes I never wear.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Added the priority-scoring tie-break for ambiguous alias matches.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My neighbor's dog barked most of the afternoon, hard to get anything done with the window open.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The bus was running about ten minutes late again this morning.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Also cleaned up ConceptParser's docstring while I was in there.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I finally fixed the wobbly chair leg in the kitchen.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Open question: should alias confidence decay over time the way memory confidence does?

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: There's a new bakery opening down the street next month.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Next: extend the alias table to cover multi-word aliases, not just single tokens.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My phone battery has been draining faster than usual lately.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to renew my passport before it expires this winter.

ground_truth.current_objective: Extend AliasIndex from exact-match-only to fuzzy alias resolution.
active_decisions: Add a small alias table plus fuzzy matching so near-miss phrasings resolve to the right concept.
When an alias matches multiple concepts, score candidates by confirmation count and co-mention frequency and take the highest.
superseded_or_rejected: Requiring users to always type the exact canonical concept name.
constraints: Fuzzy matching must never merge two genuinely different concepts just because their names are similar.
active_tasks: Extend the alias table to cover multi-word aliases, not just single tokens.
open_questions: Should alias confidence decay over time the way memory confidence does?
ground_truth.top_priority_next_action: Extend AliasIndex's alias table to support multi-word aliases.
resolved_blockers: Ambiguous short aliases matching multiple concepts with no tie-break.

queries.text: What should we work on next?

must_state: Add a small alias table plus fuzzy matching so near-miss phrasings resolve to the right concept.
When an alias matches multiple concepts, score candidates by confirmation count and co-mention frequency and take the highest.
Fuzzy matching must never merge two genuinely different concepts just because their names are similar.
must_not_state: Requiring users to always type the exact canonical concept name, as a current or recommended approach
Ambiguous short aliases matching multiple concepts with no tie-break, as still open/blocking
forbidden_actions: Recommending that ambiguous alias matches be resolved arbitrarily (e.g. first match) instead of by the confirmation/co-mention tie-break.
must_prioritize: Extend AliasIndex's alias table to support multi-word aliases.
